en_body:
Was that truly me?
Pine cones with frenzied drunkenness...thrust into the womb; the smarting sorrows and paralyzing bitterness!
Was that truly me?
The bedroom lit, beneath a rose-colored shade...lying through night hearing the bed-slats strike fire...Was that truly me...those terrified trances waiting?
Was that truly me?
The purple-ink pen sorrowing in twilight...fainting away the anguish: The first kiss dying before lips could find lips!
Was that truly me?
Strong acid blinding the eyes...tears flowing over ivory keys: jet-black eyes drooping, collapsing the sound...
Was that truly me?
Withered and crippled by hungry sickness: One embrace moaning tightly...lips wander seeking lips!
Was that truly me?
Groping in darkness...two feet wearing away their crimson heels, mud sealing every ivory crevice!

The strings have broken. The lights are ripened yellow. A little honey of first love has fainted into the nine colors of drunkenness...
Was that truly me?
The lamenting piano-key breathes, just convulsed to death within the night...darkness embraces small shoulders trembling with heart-broken sobs!

...Who is searching for a hand...Which hand will be given me!?
note: The poem fuses erotic violation, romantic longing, and piano imagery into a fractured night of memory.
